Overview

Address
3427 CENTRAL AVENUE NORTHEAST, MINNEAPOLIS, MN 55418

6127889757

75 (78% of beds filled)

For-profit corporation
Provider type

Medicare/Medicaid since
1975-03-01
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 2.03 2.53 3.10 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.45 0.44 0.68 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.51 0.56 0.78 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.07 1.52 1.64 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 0.96 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.00 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
1.76
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.36
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.68
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
33.3%
annual
RN turnover
30.0%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
0.90
lower acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.65
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-04-17 31 19 13 318 1 318
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-03-07 35 12 24 275 1 275
Total weighted health score
307.3
lower is better

Deficiencies (83)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

15 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
61 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
2 G/H/I Actual harm
5 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. G 2025-12-19 2026-01-23
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-12-19 2026-01-23
0805 Ensure each resident receives and the facility provides food prepared in a form designed to meet individual needs. J 2025-12-15 2025-12-04
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2025-11-21 2025-12-07
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. G 2025-11-21 2025-12-07
0636 Assess the resident completely in a timely manner when first admitted, and then periodically, at least every 12 months. D 2025-11-20 2025-12-07
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. J 2025-08-27 2025-09-30
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-08-27 2025-09-30
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2025-08-27 2025-09-30
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-08-27 2025-09-30
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2025-08-27 2025-09-30
0577 Allow residents to easily view the nursing home's survey results and communicate with advocate agencies. C 2025-04-17 2025-06-06
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-04-17 2025-06-06
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. B 2025-04-17 2025-06-06
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-04-17 2025-06-06
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-04-17 2025-06-06
0660 Plan the resident's discharge to meet the resident's goals and needs. D 2025-04-17 2025-06-06
0676 Ensure residents do not lose the ability to perform activities of daily living unless there is a medical reason. D 2025-04-17 2025-06-06
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-04-17 2025-06-06
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. D 2025-04-17 2025-06-06
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-04-17 2025-06-06
0712 Ensure that the resident and his/her doctor meet face-to-face at all required visits. D 2025-04-17 2025-06-06
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-04-17 2025-06-06
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2025-04-17 2025-06-06
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2025-04-17 2025-06-06
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2025-04-17 2025-06-06
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2025-04-17 2025-06-06
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. D 2025-04-17 2025-06-06
0912 Provide rooms that are at least 80 square feet per resident in multiple rooms and 100 square feet for single resident rooms. B 2025-04-17 —
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2025-04-17 2025-06-06
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-03-31 2025-04-18
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2024-10-16 2024-11-22
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-10-16 2024-11-22
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-10-16 2024-11-22
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2024-10-16 2024-10-01
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. F 2024-10-16 2024-11-22
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. F 2024-10-16 2024-11-22
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2024-09-06 2024-10-25
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. J 2024-09-06 2024-10-25
0712 Ensure that the resident and his/her doctor meet face-to-face at all required visits. B 2024-09-06 2024-10-25
0714 Ensure the physician properly assigns and delegates tasks to a physician assistant, nurse practitioner or clinical nurse specialist. C 2024-09-06 2024-10-25
0715 Ensure the physician properly assigns and delegates tasks to a qualified dietitian (or other qualified nutrition professional); or to a qualified therapist. C 2024-09-06 2024-10-25
0837 Establish a governing body that is legally responsible for establishing and implementing policies for managing and operating the facility and appoints a properly licensed administrator responsible for managing the facility. C 2024-09-06 2024-10-25
0841 Designate a physician to serve as medical director responsible for implementation of resident care policies and coordination of medical care in the facility. C 2024-09-06 2024-10-25
0844 Follow rules about disclosure of ownership requirements and tell the state agency about changes in ownership and/or administrative personnel. C 2024-09-06 2024-10-25
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-06-26 2024-07-17
0567 Honor the resident's right to manage his or her financial affairs. E 2024-04-11 2024-05-10
0568 Properly hold, secure, and manage each resident's personal money which is deposited with the nursing home. E 2024-04-11 2024-05-10
0553 Allow resident to participate in the development and implementation of his or her person-centered plan of care. E 2024-03-07 2024-04-29
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2024-03-07 2024-04-29

  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.
